title: Ultimately, Sam Altman Revealed What Techno-Oligarchs Really Think About Us Humans
author: Srgg
text:
Member-only story
Ultimately, Sam Altman Revealed What Techno-Oligarchs Really Think About Us Humans
I owe the reader an apology if this article comes across as overly emotional. I tried to strike a balance, though I’m not sure how well I succeeded. Still, I hope I managed to get my arguments across.
Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI, has finally said it out loud. In an interview at the Express Adda event in India, he stated:
One of the things that is always unfair in this comparison is … people talk about how much energy it takes to train an AI model relative to how much it costs a human to do one inference query. But it also takes a lot of energy to train a human. It takes like 20 years of life and all of the food you eat during that time before you get smart.
From Altman’s perspective, this is a rational argument. He justifies the resource consumption required to train AI models by comparing it to the resources we humans consume.
For him, AI and humanity belong to the same value category. He measures both in terms of utility.
But not everyone thinks like Sam Altman. Immanuel Kant, the great German philosopher, once said:
